questionText: Provide details on the commodities relevant to the metals production activities of your organization.
questionKo: details on the commodities relevant to the metals production activities of 귀사.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Unlike most other high-impact sectors identified by CDP, the metals and mining sector is heterogeneous and highly diverse in structure, and can even be characterized as a group of disparate sub-sectors. It is, therefore, problematic to consider sector-wide intensities. However, it is useful to identify metals and mining commodities individually and shed light on the presence and impact of them within and between organizations.

columns.text: Annual production in copper-equivalent units (thousand tons)
columns.textKo: Annual production in copper-equivalent units (thousand tons)

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select one of the options under the "Processing metals" sub-heading in 1.17.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select one 의 options under the "프로세스ing metals" sub-heading in 1.17.

guidance.text:
General 
Complete the table for each metal output product selected in column 1. 
Your production and capacity figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 
If you did not have any capacity or production in the reporting year, then enter 0 (zero) in the relevant field. 
Negative numbers are not allowed. 
To add more rows to the table, please use the "Add Row" button to the bottom right. 
You should apply the same logic to your boundary definition as provided in question 7.19. 
Output product (column 1) 
Select the product(s) relevant to your organization's metal production activities. Select as many as applicable. You may also wish to select those with no production in the reporting year to confirm zero activity. 
If you select "Other non-ferrous metals (Please specify)" or "Other ferrous metals (Please specify)", provide a label for the output product. 
Capacity (metric tons) (column 2) 
Enter the production capacity in metric tons for the metal product selected in column 1. 
Production, metric tons (column 3) 
Enter the production in metric tons for the metal product selected in column 1. 
Production, copper-equivalent units, metric tons (column 4) 
Enter the production in copper-equivalent units for the metal product selected in column 1. 
Share the methodology used for this equivalent in column 7. 
Scope 1 emissions (column 5) 
Enter the Scope 1 emissions associated with the metal product selected in column 1. 
Scope 2 emissions (column 6) 
Enter the Scope 2 emissions associated with the metal product selected in column 1. 
Scope 2 emissions approach (column 7) 
This column only appears if you select "We are reporting a Scope 2, market-based figure" in column "Scope 2, market-based" of 7.3. 
Pricing methodology for copper-equivalent figure (column 8) 
Disclose the formula(e) and methodology used to calculate the copper-equivalent unit reported in column 4. 
Comment (column 9) (optional) 
Use this column to provide any additional information relevant to the metal product selected in column 1.
guidance.textKo: 일반 Complete the table for each metal output product 선택 in 열 1. Your production 및 capacity figures 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 만약 you did not have 하나 이상 capacity 또는 production in the 보고연도, then enter 0 (zero) in the 관련 field. Negative numbers are not allowed. To add more 행 to the table, please use the "Add 행" button to the bottom right. You 해야 합니다 apply the same logic to your boundary definition as 입력 in 문항 7.19. Output product (열 1) Select the product(s) 관련 to 귀사의 metal production activities. Select as many as 해당되는 경우. You may also wish to select those with no production in the 보고연도 to confirm zero activity. 만약 you select "Other non-ferrous metals (직접 입력)" 또는 "Other ferrous metals (직접 입력)", provide a label 에 대한 output product. Capacity (metric tons) (열 2) Enter the production capacity in metric tons 에 대한 metal product 선택 in 열 1. Production, metric tons (열 3) Enter the production in metric tons 에 대한 metal product 선택 in 열 1. Production, copper-equivalent units, metric tons (열 4) Enter the production in copper-equivalent units 에 대한 metal product 선택 in 열 1. Share the 방법론 used for this equivalent in 열 7. Scope 1 배출량 (열 5) Enter the Scope 1 배출량 associated with the metal product 선택 in 열 1. Scope 2 배출량 (열 6) Enter the Scope 2 배출량 associated with the metal product 선택 in 열 1. Scope 2 배출량 approach (열 7) This 열 only appears 만약 you select "We are reporting a Scope 2, 시장기반 figure" in 열 "Scope 2, 시장기반" of 7.3. Pricing 방법론 for copper-equivalent figure (열 8) Disclose the formula(e) 및 방법론 used to calculate the copper-equivalent unit 보고 in 열 4. Comment (열 9) (optional) Use this 열 to provide 하나 이상 additional information 관련 to the metal product 선택 in 열 1.

guidance.text:
Copper-equivalent units: Metal equivalent calculations are used to compare similar deposits with slightly different metal ratios. Metal-equivalent units allow organizations to assess how much their deposits are worth in terms of just one of the metals resources they have. Copper-equivalent units enable investors to understand how much combined metal deposits are worth in terms of copper.
Metals production activities (or processing of metals): refer to the production of finished metal commodities, resulting from a series of operations that transform raw materials.
Platinum group metals: Platinum group metals are six transitional metals located in the d-block of the period table (groups 8, 9 and 10, within periods 5 and 6), that are chemically, physically, and anatomically similar. These metals are ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, osmium, iridium, and platinum.
guidance.textKo: Copper-equivalent units: Metal equivalent 계산s are used to compare similar deposits with slightly different metal ratios. Metal-equivalent units allow organizations to assess how much their deposits are worth in terms of just one 의 metals resources they have. Copper-equivalent units enable investors to understand how much combined metal deposits are worth in terms of copper. Metals production activities (or 프로세스ing of metals): refer to the production of finished metal commodities, resulting from a series of operations that transform raw materials. Platinum group metals: Platinum group metals are six transitional metals located in the d-block 의 period table (groups 8, 9 및 10, within periods 5 및 6), that are chemically, physically, 및 anatomically similar. These metals are ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, osmium, iridium, 및 platinum.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 8/8 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 8/8점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완성 - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: Unlike most other high-impact sectors identified by CDP, the metals 및 mining sector is heterogeneous 및 highly diverse in structure, 및 can even be characterized as a group of disparate sub-sectors. It is, therefore, problematic to consider sector-wide intensities. However, it is useful to identify metals 및 mining commodities individually 및 shed light on the presence 및 impact 의m within 및 between organizations.
